patch:
diff --git b/Service/Correlation_Decisioning.py a/Service/Correlation_Decisioning.py
index c9134f8..3ff4872 100644
--- b/Service/Correlation_Decisioning.py
+++ a/Service/Correlation_Decisioning.py
@@ -110,9 +110,9 @@ class CorrelationDecision:
             # Check if all words in source name are present in target name or vice versa
             common_words = source_name_words.intersection(target_name_words)
 
-            if len(common_words) == len(source_name_words) and len(common_words) == len(target_name_words):
+            if len(common_words) == len(source_name_words) or len(common_words) == len(target_name_words):
                 name_score = 100  # Full match (all words match)
-            elif len(common_words) > 1:
+            elif len(common_words) > 0:
                 name_score = 70  # Partial match (some words match)
             else:
                 name_score = 30  # No significant match
@@ -124,7 +124,7 @@ class CorrelationDecision:
         syntactic_similarity = target['correlation_details'].get('syntactic_similarity', 0)
 
         # Scale semantic similarity score out of 100
-        if 0 <= semantic_similarity < 1:
+        if 0 <= semantic_similarity <= 1:
             semantic_score = semantic_similarity * 100  # You can adjust the scaling if needed
         # else:
         #     semantic_score = 0  # Default score if not in valid range
@@ -150,7 +150,7 @@ class CorrelationDecision:
     @staticmethod
     def is_valid_correlation(source, target, threshold=70):
         score = CorrelationDecision.calculate_score(source, target)
-        return score > threshold, score
+        return score >= threshold, score
 
     @staticmethod
     def process_json(json_data, threshold=70):
diff --git b/Service/Get_Transactions_Custom_Logic.py a/Service/Get_Transactions_Custom_Logic.py
index 5f16100..92dd7e7 100644
--- b/Service/Get_Transactions_Custom_Logic.py
+++ a/Service/Get_Transactions_Custom_Logic.py
@@ -100,7 +100,7 @@ class GetTransactionsCustom:
         if day and month_name and year:
             # Get the appropriate suffix for the day
             day = int(day)
-            if 4 <= day <= 20 or 23 <= day <= 30:
+            if 4 <= day <= 20 or 24 <= day <= 30:
                 suffix = "th"
             else:
                 suffix = ["st", "nd", "rd"][day % 10 - 1]
test_patch:
diff --git a/tests/test_correlation_boundaries.py b/tests/test_correlation_boundaries.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..6b42d34
--- /dev/null
+++ b/tests/test_correlation_boundaries.py
@@ -0,0 +1,152 @@
+"""Boundary / edge-case correctness for the correlation scoring and formatting math.
+
+These assert the correct outputs on exact-threshold, extreme-of-range and
+full-containment inputs that ordinary correlation traffic never exercises: a
+day-of-month sitting on the seam of the ordinal-suffix table, a pair of names
+where one side's tokens are entirely contained in the other, two names that
+share a single token, a similarity at the very top of its valid range, and a
+correlation score that lands exactly on the accept threshold. Everyday inputs
+sit comfortably inside these ranges, so a boundary regression here stays
+invisible to them.
+"""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import unittest
+
+from Service.Correlation_Decisioning import CorrelationDecision
+from Service.Get_Transactions_Custom_Logic import GetTransactionsCustom
+
+
+def _record(name=None, semantic=0.0, syntactic=0.0):
+    """A minimal transaction record for the decisioning scorer.
+
+    No amount / date is supplied, so only the name factor (when a name is
+    given) and the always-present semantic + syntactic factors contribute.
+    """
+    metadata = {}
+    if name is not None:
+        metadata["name"] = name
+    return {
+        "amount": "",
+        "date": "",
+        "date_copy": {},
+        "metadata": metadata,
+        "correlation_details": {
+            "semantic_similarity": semantic,
+            "syntactic_similarity": syntactic,
+        },
+    }
+
+
+class TestDayOrdinalSuffix(unittest.TestCase):
+    def test_twenty_third_uses_rd_suffix(self) -> None:
+        # 23 ends in 3 -> ordinal "rd" ("23rd"), like 3rd and 21st/22nd; it is
+        # not part of the 24..30 run that all take "th".
+        self.assertEqual(
+            GetTransactionsCustom.format_date({"day": 23, "month": 7, "year": 2024}),
+            "23rd July 2024",
+        )
+
+    def test_first_uses_st_suffix(self) -> None:
+        self.assertEqual(
+            GetTransactionsCustom.format_date({"day": 1, "month": 7, "year": 2024}),
+            "1st July 2024",
+        )
+
+    def test_twenty_first_uses_st_suffix(self) -> None:
+        self.assertEqual(
+            GetTransactionsCustom.format_date({"day": 21, "month": 7, "year": 2024}),
+            "21st July 2024",
+        )
+
+    def test_twenty_fourth_uses_th_suffix(self) -> None:
+        self.assertEqual(
+            GetTransactionsCustom.format_date({"day": 24, "month": 7, "year": 2024}),
+            "24th July 2024",
+        )
+
+    def test_thirtieth_uses_th_suffix(self) -> None:
+        self.assertEqual(
+            GetTransactionsCustom.format_date({"day": 30, "month": 7, "year": 2024}),
+            "30th July 2024",
+        )
+
+    def test_month_and_year_only(self) -> None:
+        self.assertEqual(
+            GetTransactionsCustom.format_date({"day": None, "month": 7, "year": 2024}),
+            "July 2024",
+        )
+
+
+class TestNameFullMatch(unittest.TestCase):
+    def test_contained_name_is_full_match(self) -> None:
+        # Every token of the source name appears in the target name (source is a
+        # subset of target): all of the source's words match, so this is a full
+        # name match (100), not a partial one.
+        source = _record(name="john")
+        target = _record(name="john smith")
+        self.assertAlmostEqual(CorrelationDecision.calculate_score(source, target), 100.0 / 3)
+
+    def test_identical_names_is_full_match(self) -> None:
+        source = _record(name="john smith")
+        target = _record(name="john smith")
+        self.assertAlmostEqual(CorrelationDecision.calculate_score(source, target), 100.0 / 3)
+
+
+class TestNamePartialMatch(unittest.TestCase):
+    def test_single_shared_token_is_partial_match(self) -> None:
+        # The names share exactly one token and neither is contained in the
+        # other: some words match, so this scores as a partial match (70).
+        source = _record(name="john smith")
+        target = _record(name="john doe")
+        self.assertAlmostEqual(CorrelationDecision.calculate_score(source, target), 70.0 / 3)
+
+    def test_two_shared_tokens_is_partial_match(self) -> None:
+        source = _record(name="john michael smith")
+        target = _record(name="john michael doe")
+        self.assertAlmostEqual(CorrelationDecision.calculate_score(source, target), 70.0 / 3)
+
+    def test_disjoint_names_no_match(self) -> None:
+        source = _record(name="alice cooper")
+        target = _record(name="bob dylan")
+        self.assertAlmostEqual(CorrelationDecision.calculate_score(source, target), 30.0 / 3)
+
+
+class TestSemanticScaling(unittest.TestCase):
+    def test_perfect_semantic_similarity_scales_to_full(self) -> None:
+        # A semantic similarity of exactly 1.0 is the maximum of the [0, 1]
+        # range and must scale to 100, not be treated as out of range.
+        source = _record(semantic=1.0, syntactic=0.6)
+        target = _record(semantic=1.0, syntactic=0.6)
+        self.assertAlmostEqual(CorrelationDecision.calculate_score(source, target), 80.0)
+
+    def test_midrange_similarities_scale_linearly(self) -> None:
+        source = _record(semantic=0.8, syntactic=0.6)
+        target = _record(semantic=0.8, syntactic=0.6)
+        self.assertAlmostEqual(CorrelationDecision.calculate_score(source, target), 70.0)
+
+
+class TestCorrelationThreshold(unittest.TestCase):
+    def test_score_exactly_on_threshold_is_valid(self) -> None:
+        # A score sitting exactly on the accept threshold meets it and must be
+        # reported as a valid correlation.
+        source = _record(semantic=0.7, syntactic=0.7)
+        target = _record(semantic=0.7, syntactic=0.7)
+        is_valid, score = CorrelationDecision.is_valid_correlation(source, target)
+        self.assertAlmostEqual(score, 70.0)
+        self.assertTrue(is_valid)
+
+    def test_score_above_threshold_is_valid(self) -> None:
+        source = _record(semantic=0.9, syntactic=0.9)
+        is_valid, score = CorrelationDecision.is_valid_correlation(source, source)
+        self.assertTrue(is_valid)
+
+    def test_score_below_threshold_is_invalid(self) -> None:
+        source = _record(semantic=0.5, syntactic=0.5)
+        is_valid, score = CorrelationDecision.is_valid_correlation(source, source)
+        self.assertFalse(is_valid)
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    unittest.main()
